key_insights.insight: Our current societal unhappiness stems from an imbalance, prioritizing autonomy over human connection.
key_insights.quote: The big thing that I think we're getting wrong is the balance that we maintain between autonomy and connection...the big thing that we've got wrong right now is doing what I want to do right now rather than connecting.

key_insights.insight: Modern city living, while offering opportunities, contributes to unhappiness by promoting isolation and autonomy.
key_insights.quote: Cities are all about autonomy...If I go to a city, I can get any kind of education I want. I can have any kind of job that I want...But I'm going to pay a price...We become more isolated.

key_insights.insight: While money can increase happiness in the short term, societal wealth does not correlate with overall happiness, as status becomes the focus.
key_insights.quote: Past $600,000 a year, money still makes you happier...But there's this thing known as the Easterland paradox, and that is that as society gets richer, people get no happier.

key_insights.insight: Honest signals of quality, such as risk-taking, humor, and kindness, are what attract potential mates from an evolutionary perspective.
key_insights.quote: What you want are what we call honest signals of quality...one of the clearest honest signals for men to demonstrate for women is what is risk taking...Humor is an honest signal of quality...as humans, we also care an enormous amount about kindness.

key_insights.insight: Pornography's rise and social media induced laziness are contributing to a decline in sexual activity amongst young people.
key_insights.quote: Their actual sexual behavior is going down. They're having less sex than they were 20 years ago... what's going up is pornography watching...social media induced laziness in our social habits.

key_insights.insight: Falling fertility rates in industrialized countries result from separating sex from reproduction and the increasing difficulties associated with raising children.
key_insights.quote: Fertility rates are going way down...that's simply because we never evolved to want children. We evolved to want sex...And in a world without the pill, that automatically resulted in children.

key_insights.insight: Engaging in activities that enhance experiences rather than acquiring things tends to lead to greater happiness.
key_insights.quote: If you have disposable income, it makes you a lot happier when you spend it on things to do rather than things to have...things to have are a status game, and that status game is impossible to win.

key_insights.insight: Men who have more 'gay genes', but are still straight, are more attractive to women.
key_insights.quote: Men who have more gay genes but are still straight have more sexual partners than men who have fewer gay genes and are also straight. So being a little bit gay is super attractive to women.

actionable_advice.advice: Match your connection need with your autonomy need by finding activities you enjoy doing alone and incorporating others.
actionable_advice.how_to: If you enjoy crossword puzzles, do them with a friend or family member. If you enjoy running, join a running group.

actionable_advice.advice: Set rules for reintroducing connection so you don't have to decide to do it every time, making it more likely to become habitual.
actionable_advice.how_to: Decide to call your sister to do a puzzle together after breakfast, as automatically as you brush your teeth.

actionable_advice.advice: Set an end date or specific, measurable goals for entrepreneurial pursuits to prevent endless striving at the cost of relationships.
actionable_advice.how_to: Define what success means in terms of money earned or time invested, and establish criteria for both success and loss before beginning the venture.

actionable_advice.advice: If having a 'naughty day' where you are going to indulge in things that negatively impact health, try to remain sedentary.
actionable_advice.how_to: If you're going to be drinking alcohol, avoid exercising that same day because it may amplify the alcohol's negative effect.

memorable_stories: Von Hippel recounts how visiting a wealthy friend and reading about the Hadza tribe led him to realize he, like his friend, was failing to appreciate the benefits of modern life compared to hunter-gatherers.
Von Hippel shares an anecdote about a bet he made with his team regarding YouTube subscribers and how it could end up costing him a lot of money.
contrarian_claims: Humans never evolved to want children, they evolved to want sex, and that sex led to children.
Robots could be better nannies than humans because they have infinite patience and medical knowledge, and would never abuse a child.
It is okay to be lonely when you are younger and pursuing big dreams as an entrepreneur; you can prioritize connecting more when you are older.
For hunter-gatherers, they actually chose the nice guys over the better hunters as partners to go out on hunts.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Behavioural Psychologist: We're Not Having Enough Sex! Fat Makes You Attractive & The Poorer You Are The Friendlier You Are!
